data.title: Annaly Capital Q4 earnings beat as spreads tighten, servicing income rises
data.description:
Annaly Capital Management (NLY) turned in slightly stronger-than-expected Q4 earnings, helped by spread tightening and strong fund flows, the company said on Wednesday. Net servicing income also showed healthy growth.

Q4 EPS for distribution of $0.74 per share, topping the average analyst estimate of $0.73, rose from $0.73 in Q3 and $ in Q4 2024.

Book value per common share of $20.21 at Dec. 31, 2025, exceeding the Visible Alpha estimate of $19.65, increased from $19.25 at Sept. 30, 2025.

Economic return of 8.6% improved from 6.1% in the prior quarter. Economic leverage of 5.6x edged down from 5.7x in Q3.

"Our agency business benefited from meaningful spread tightening, supported by strong fund flows, improved fundamentals and a more favorable operating environment," said CEO and Co-Chief Investment Officer David Finkelstein. "Our residential credit platform generated record production from our whole loan correspondent channel and securitization platform, which continues to gain market share through the introduction of innovative structured transactions."

The mortgage REIT’s total assets of $135.6B at the end of Q4 climbed from $125.9B at the end of Q3 and $103.6B at the end of 2024.

Its total portfolio of $104.7B included $92.9B in highly liquid agency portfolio. Annaly’s (NLY) agency portfolio increased by 6%, representing 62% of dedicated capital, with accretive capital raised during the quarter predominantly deployed in 5.0% coupon TBA and generic collateral securities, higher coupon specified pools and agency CMBS.

Q4 net interest income of $366.6M grew from $275.8M in Q3 and $187.3M in Q4 2024. Net interest margin, excluding PAA, of % vs. 1.70% in the previous quarter and % a year ago.

Net interest spread, excluding PAA, was 1.49% compared with 1.50% in Q3 and 1.47% in the prior year’s Q4.

Net servicing income of $139.6M increased from $126.3M in the prior quarter and $115.6M a year ago.

The average economic cost of interest-bearing liabilities was 3.95% at Dec. 31, 2025, vs. 3.96% at Sept. 30, 2025, and 3.79% at Dec. 31, 2024.

Total general and administrative expenses were $51.2M vs. $50.3M in the prior quarter and $44.0M a year ago.

Conference call on Jan. 29 at 9:00 AM ET.

data.title: KB Home names McGibney CEO, Mezger to become Executive Chairman
data.description:
• KB Home (KBH) on Wednesday said that its board has appointed Robert McGibney as President and CEO, effective March 1.
  • McGibney will succeed Jeffrey Mezger, who will become the company’s first Executive Chairman after serving as Chief Executive Officer since 2006 and Chairman since 2016.
  • The company said McGibney, a long-serving executive, will also join the board on March 1 and will be nominated for election at KB Home’s 2026 annual meeting of shareholders.
  • KBH -0.0% after hours to $57.95.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Century Communities Non-GAAP EPS of $1.59 beats by $0.20, revenue of $1.2B beats by $150M
data.description:
• Century Communities press release (CCS): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.59 beats by $0.20.
  • Revenue of $1.2B (-7.7% Y/Y) beats by $150M.

  • Full Year 2026 Outlook

    Scott Dixon, Chief Financial Officer of the Company, commented, "Assuming no significant changes to the current economic environment, we currently expect our full year 2026 new home deliveries to be in the range of 10,000 to 11,000 homes and our homes sales revenues to be in the range of $3.6 billion to $4.1 billion. Our current guidance reflects an increase in our average open communities in the mid-single digit percentage range and similar per community absorption levels as the back half of 2025."

data.title: Las Vegas Sands Non-GAAP EPS of $0.85 beats by $0.08, revenue of $3.65B beats by $310M
data.description:
• Las Vegas Sands press release (LVS): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.85 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $3.65B (+25.9% Y/Y) beats by $310M.

  • Consolidated adjusted property EBITDA was $1.41 billion, compared to $1.11 billion in the prior year quarter.

    Full year 2025 operating income was $2.82 billion, compared to $2.40 billion in 2024.  Net income attributable to Las Vegas Sands (LVS) was $1.63 billion, or $2.35 per diluted share, in 2025.  This compared to $1.45 billion, or $1.96 per diluted share, in 2024.

    Sands China Ltd. Consolidated Financial ResultsOn a GAAP basis, total net revenues for SCL increased 16.4% to $2.05 billion, compared to the fourth quarter of 2024.  Net income for SCL was $213 million, compared to $237 million in the fourth quarter of 2024.

    On a GAAP basis, 2025 total net revenues for SCL increased 5.1% to $7.44 billion, compared to 2024.  Net income for SCL was $901 million in 2025, compared to $1.05 billion in 2024.
